about.title: About Our Calculators
about.paragraph1: Calcify provides a comprehensive collection of free online calculators designed to simplify complex financial and everyday calculations. Whether you're planning a home purchase, filing your taxes, or managing investments, our tools help you make informed decisions quickly and accurately.
about.paragraph2: Each calculator is built with precision in mind, using up-to-date formulas and current regulations. Our platform supports multiple currencies and formats, making it useful for users around the world. All calculations are performed locally in your browser for maximum privacy and security.
about.paragraph3: From mortgage and loan calculations to tax estimations and investment projections, we cover a wide range of financial scenarios. Our intuitive interface makes it easy to adjust parameters and see real-time results, helping you explore different options and outcomes.
about.disclaimer: Disclaimer: These calculators are for informational and educational purposes only. They should not be considered as financial advice. Always consult with a qualified professional for financial or legal matters.

about.title: About the VAT calculator
about.paragraph: VAT (Value Added Tax), also known as GST or sales tax in some countries, is a consumption tax applied to goods and services. This calculator helps you add VAT to a net amount or extract VAT from a gross amount using the standard rate for your chosen country.

formulas.title: Formulas
formulas.addFormula: Amount with VAT = Amount without VAT × (1 + Rate / 100)
formulas.excludeFormula: Amount without VAT = Amount with VAT ÷ (1 + Rate / 100). VAT amount = Amount with VAT − Amount without VAT.

ru.rates10: 10% — food, children's goods, medical products, printed matter.
ru.rates0: 0% — exports, international transport, and certain other operations.
ru.usn: Simplified tax system (USN): businesses on USN are generally not VAT payers (with exceptions). Thresholds and rules may change; check current legislation for 2025–2026.

examples.title: Calculation examples
examples.ex1: Example 1: 1,000 net at 20% VAT → 1,000 × 1.20 = 1,200 gross; VAT amount = 200.
examples.ex2: Example 2: 2,400 gross at 20% VAT → 2,400 ÷ 1.20 = 2,000 net; VAT = 400.
examples.ex3: Example 3: 500 net at 10% (reduced rate) → 500 × 1.10 = 550 gross; VAT = 50.

howToUse.title: How to use the calculator
howToUse.step1: Select country to use the standard VAT rate (or enter a custom rate).
howToUse.step2: Choose mode: add VAT to net amount or extract VAT from gross amount.
howToUse.step3: Enter the amount; the result updates automatically.
howToUse.step4: Use the table below for reference rates by country.

vat.keywords: Popular searches: VAT calculator, add VAT, remove VAT, VAT rate by country, gross to net, net to gross.

autoLoan.title: Auto Loan Calculator: monthly payment and repayment schedule
autoLoan.intro: Use this auto loan calculator to estimate monthly payment, total interest and overpayment. The tool supports calculations by car cost or loan amount, with down payment, residual payment (balloon), and both annuity and differentiated payment methods.
autoLoan.formulaVars: M is monthly payment, P is principal, r is monthly interest rate (annual rate / 12), and n is number of months. For differentiated payments principal is paid in equal shares, while interest decreases each month.
autoLoan.example1: Example 1: car cost 30,000, down payment 6,000, rate 9%, term 60 months, annuity. Loan amount = 24,000. Monthly payment is about 498, total overpayment is about 5,880.
autoLoan.example2: Example 2: loan amount 20,000, residual payment 5,000, rate 11%, term 48 months, differentiated. First payment is highest, then decreases monthly; residual payment is paid at the end.
autoLoan.step1: Choose calculation mode: by car cost or by loan amount.
autoLoan.step2: Enter down payment, residual payment (if needed), interest rate, term, and payment type.
autoLoan.step3: Review monthly payment, total interest, and repayment schedule by month or year.

fuelConsumption.title: Fuel consumption calculator: per 100 km and trip cost
fuelConsumption.intro: Calculate fuel used for a trip from average consumption and distance, or find average consumption per 100 km from distance and fuel used. Metric (L/100 km, km/L) and imperial (mpg) units supported. Enter price per liter to get trip cost.
fuelConsumption.step1: Choose mode: «Trip fuel used» (you know average consumption and distance) or «Average per 100 km» (you know distance and fuel used).
fuelConsumption.step2: Select units (metric or imperial), enter values. Optionally enter price per liter to see trip cost.

refinancing.title: Refinancing calculator
refinancing.description: Compare your current loan with a new offer: see how much you save in interest and time by refinancing. The calculator uses the same repayment logic as the main loan calculator and shows side-by-side schedules.
refinancing.howItWorks: You enter the remaining principal, current rate, and term of the existing loan, plus the new loan rate and term. Interest and total repayment are computed for both; the difference is your potential savings.
refinancing.example1: Example 1: 100,000 left at 15% for 24 months. Total interest ≈ 16,100. Refinance to 10% for 24 months → interest ≈ 10,600. Savings ≈ 5,500.
refinancing.example2: Example 2: 500,000 left at 12% for 36 months. Refinance to 9% for 36 months reduces total interest by roughly 24,000.
refinancing.step1: Enter the remaining balance and current loan rate and term.
refinancing.step2: Set the new loan rate and term (and any fees if applicable).
refinancing.step3: Compare total interest and repayment schedule of both options.
refinancing.step4: Check the charts for principal vs interest and repayment over time.
refinancing.keywords: Popular searches: refinancing calculator, compare loan rates, refinance savings, switch mortgage calculator, lower interest rate comparison.

inflationBlock.title: Inflation calculator
inflationBlock.description: See how inflation erodes the purchasing power of money over time. Enter a starting amount and annual inflation rate to get the equivalent value in future years and the total inflation index.
inflationBlock.formula: Value after n years = Initial amount × (1 + rate/100)^n. The inflation index is (1 + rate/100)^n; the table shows year-by-year growth.
inflationBlock.example1: Example 1: 10,000 at 5% for 10 years → 10,000 × 1.05^10 ≈ 16,289; purchasing power halves in about 14 years at 5%.
inflationBlock.step1: Enter the initial amount and the annual inflation rate (%).
inflationBlock.step2: Set the number of years to project.
inflationBlock.step3: Read the equivalent value and inflation index in the results and table.
inflationBlock.keywords: Popular searches: inflation calculator, purchasing power, inflation rate, money value over time, inflation index.

microloan.title: Microloan calculator
microloan.description: Calculate the cost of a short-term microloan: interest and late-payment penalties. Enter the principal, annual rate, term, and optional penalty rate to see the repayment schedule and total to repay.
microloan.howItWorks: Interest is applied to the principal over the loan term. If you add a penalty rate, it is applied per day on the overdue amount after the due date. Total to repay = principal + interest + penalties (if any).
microloan.example1: Example 1: 5,000 at 1% per day for 14 days → interest depends on the method (e.g. simple); total repayment is shown in the schedule.
microloan.example2: Example 2: Same loan with 0.5% daily penalty and 5 days late → penalty accrues on the outstanding amount for 5 days.
microloan.step1: Enter the loan amount, annual interest rate, and term (days or months).
microloan.step2: Optionally set a penalty rate for late payment.
microloan.step3: View the schedule and total interest and total to repay.
microloan.keywords: Popular searches: microloan calculator, short-term loan calculator, loan interest calculator, late payment penalty calculator.

creditEarlyRepayment.title: Early repayment calculator
creditEarlyRepayment.description: See how making extra payments or a lump sum reduces your loan term and total interest. The calculator shows the impact of one-time or recurring early repayments on the schedule and total cost.
creditEarlyRepayment.howItWorks: Extra payments reduce the principal, so less interest accrues. You can add a one-time lump sum and/or a recurring extra payment. The schedule is recalculated so that the loan is paid off sooner with less total interest.
creditEarlyRepayment.example: Loan 1,000,000 at 12% for 60 months. Without early repayment: total interest ≈ 333,000. With a one-time 100,000 at month 12: term shortens and total interest drops; the chart and table show the new schedule.
creditEarlyRepayment.step1: Enter the loan amount, rate, and term (annuity or differentiated).
creditEarlyRepayment.step2: Optionally add a one-time early repayment and/or recurring extra payment.
creditEarlyRepayment.step3: View the updated schedule, total interest saved, and charts.
creditEarlyRepayment.keywords: Popular searches: early repayment calculator, lump sum calculator, extra payment loan, reduce loan term, pay off loan faster.

mortgage.title: Mortgage calculator: monthly payments and amortization
mortgage.description: A mortgage calculator helps you estimate monthly payments and total cost before you apply. Whether you are buying a first home or refinancing, knowing your payment and interest over the life of the loan supports better decisions. This tool uses standard amortization formulas and supports both fixed-rate annuity and differentiated (linear) payment types.
mortgage.toolExplanation: A mortgage (home loan) is a long-term loan secured by the property. In most countries, banks offer fixed or variable rates; the calculator assumes a fixed annual rate. You enter the property price, down payment, interest rate, and term to get the monthly payment and a full amortization schedule. Local rules may affect fees, insurance, or tax deductions—check with your lender.
mortgage.step1: Enter the full property price (purchase price or valuation used by the lender).
mortgage.step2: Enter your down payment; the calculator shows the loan amount and down payment as a percentage.
mortgage.step3: Set the annual interest rate and loan term (in years or months).
mortgage.step4: Choose annuity (equal monthly payments) or differentiated (decreasing payments). Results and the schedule update automatically.
mortgage.formula: Annuity: M = P × [r(1+r)^n] / [(1+r)^n − 1]. Differentiated: principal part = P/n each month; interest = balance × r.
mortgage.formulaNote: M = monthly payment, P = principal, r = monthly rate (annual rate ÷ 12), n = number of months.
mortgage.countrySpecifics: Rates and terms vary by country and lender. Some jurisdictions offer tax relief on mortgage interest or first-time buyer programs. Always confirm rates, fees, and eligibility with a local advisor.
mortgage.example1: Example 1 (USD): $300,000 property, $60,000 down, 6% rate, 30 years → loan $240,000, monthly payment ≈ $1,439 (annuity), total interest ≈ $278,000.
mortgage.example2: Example 2 (EUR): €250,000 property, €50,000 down, 4% rate, 20 years → loan €200,000, monthly payment ≈ €1,212 (annuity). First payment (differentiated) ≈ €1,333, last ≈ €837.
mortgage.example3: Example 3: Same €200,000 at 4% over 15 years → monthly ≈ €1,479; total interest is lower than over 20 years despite higher payment.
mortgage.commonMistakes: Confusing gross and net price (include or exclude taxes/fees as your bank does). Forgetting insurance, property tax, or maintenance in your budget. Comparing only monthly payment without total interest. Ignoring rate type (fixed vs variable) and early-repayment rules.
mortgage.faq1q: What is the difference between annuity and differentiated payments?
mortgage.faq1a: Annuity: equal monthly payment; early on you pay more interest, later more principal. Differentiated: fixed principal part each month plus interest on the remaining balance, so payments start higher and decrease over time; total interest is usually lower.
mortgage.faq2q: Does the calculator include taxes and insurance?
mortgage.faq2a: No. It only computes principal and interest. Add property tax, home insurance, and (if applicable) mortgage insurance separately for your full monthly cost.
mortgage.faq3q: Can I use it for refinancing?
mortgage.faq3a: Yes. Enter the new loan amount (e.g. remaining balance), new rate, and new term to see the new payment and schedule.
mortgage.faq4a: The math is exact for the inputs given. Real offers may differ due to fees, rounding, or rate type (e.g. variable). Use it for comparison and planning; confirm figures with your lender.
mortgage.faq5a: Use the annual percentage rate (APR) or the nominal rate your lender uses for the repayment schedule. If you have a teaser rate, consider calculating both the initial and the follow-up rate period.
mortgage.conclusion: Use this mortgage calculator to compare scenarios, see how down payment and term affect your payment and total interest, and plan your budget. For country-specific rules and offers, consult a licensed advisor or your bank.
mortgage.keywords: Popular searches: mortgage calculator, monthly payment calculator, amortization schedule, home loan calculator, refinance calculator, annuity vs differentiated, mortgage interest calculator.
